authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(8) — Bear Creek (San Bernardino Co.)
sources.quotedText: Bear Creek (San Bernardino Co.) from Big Bear Dam to confluence of Santa Ana River. All year. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout

sources.title: CDFW — Designated Wild and Heritage Trout Waters (Inland Deserts Region listing includes Bear Creek)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 269732, "Bear Creek", Stream, San Bernardino Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":269732,"gaz_name":"Bear Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"San Bernardino"} — geometry multipoint; first point (Web Mercator -13026055.3344, 4050345.7590000033) converts to lon -117.015046, lat 34.160293, at the creek's mouth (confluence with the Santa Ana River near Seven Oaks), replacing the file's previous coordinate (-116.9800, 34.1900), which was off by ~4.6 km. Corroborated by Wikipedia's infobox mouth coordinate 34°09'37"N 117°0'54"W for GNIS ID 269732.

parameters.note: Applies to Bear Creek from Big Bear Dam downstream to the confluence with the Santa Ana River.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year, from Big Bear Dam to the confluence with the Santa Ana River
regulations.verbatimText: Bear Creek (San Bernardino Co.) from Big Bear Dam to confluence of Santa Ana River. All year.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures may be used (no bait)
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 trout daily bag limit; this special regulation entry does not specify a distinct possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
